danger_signals.visual_form: Sprite animation and entrance on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat approaching player; requires defensive positioning or pre-emptive strikes

danger_signals.visual_form: Enemy HP Display shown separately at top of screen (RAM 0x0066)
danger_signals.meaning: Boss encounter initiated; sustained threat with damage accumulation

danger_signals.visual_form: Ryu flash/knockback animation; HP meter depletion; invulnerability timer (RAM 0x0095) runs 60-0
danger_signals.meaning: World state shifted to 'damaged'; player must avoid further hits during brief invincibility window

danger_signals.visual_form: Countdown displayed; timer at RAM 0x0063 decrements when timer fractions (0x0062) = 60
danger_signals.meaning: Time pressure escalates; failure imminent if level not completed

danger_signals.signal: Fall into bottomless pit or off-screen
danger_signals.visual_form: Ryu leaves viewport vertically; no collision detected
danger_signals.meaning: Instant life loss; environmental hazard activates

danger_signals.visual_form: Specific enemy sprite with distinctive visual design
danger_signals.meaning: Critical narrative danger; father is compromised and controlled; emotional and mechanical threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Large unique sprite; fiery projectile attacks
danger_signals.meaning: Ultimate antagonist; final world-state conflict; uses fire-based aggression

safety_signals.visual_form: Screen transition; chapter/act eye-catch (Japanese version shows unique act titles: 宿命/Destiny, 脱出/Escape, 追跡/Pursuit, 罠/Trap, 死闘/Life-or-death Struggle, 降魔/Conquering the Devil)
safety_signals.meaning: World state progressed; immediate threats neutralized; safe checkpoint reached

safety_signals.visual_form: Character flashing after damage taken; invulnerability counter (RAM 0x0095)
safety_signals.meaning: Temporary protection granted; brief window of reduced danger after adversity

safety_signals.visual_form: Lantern/special weapon sprite pickup (RAM 0x04D0-0x04E7); special weapon enabled (RAM 0x00C9: $00=None, $80=Fire Wheel, $81=Throwing Star, $82=Windmill Star, $84=Invincible Fire Wheel, $85=Jump and Slash)
safety_signals.meaning: Player capability increased; world state becomes more favorable; temporary offensive advantage
Threat eliminated; section cleared; narrative progression secured

safety_signals.visual_form: Final cutscene shows rising sun; brightness palette shift
safety_signals.meaning: Darkness overcome; world state transitions to peace; story climax resolved positively

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Dojo/temple ancestry (father Ken's lineage); association with demon statues (quest objects)

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Masked figure identity; demon statue control
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Masked Devil (Ken possessed); Basaquer (statue thief); Bloody Malth (demon champion); Jaquio's true form (final confrontation)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Ancient temple in Amazon; seal chambers; fiery projectile signature; control crystal orbs

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Boss-class enemy sprites; individual attack patterns
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Encountered at end of each Act; distinct visual designs per boss
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Placed at strategic temple locations; defend inner sanctum progression

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Foster (communicator/betrayer); armed men (interrogators); Irene Lew (double agent)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Interrogation room; parachute drop zone; satellite communication; external political pressure

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Varied sprite designs (knife tossers, birds, bats, cheetahs, generic soldiers)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Sprite ID at RAM 0x0400-0x0407 determines type; grey disc-tossers have visible HP (RAM 0x0490-0x0497)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout levels; indicate Jaquio's territorial control; spawned by level design

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Jungle and outdoor environments; parachute drop location; dense vegetation; temple exterior approach
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ryu weak/unequipped; enemies are common foot soldiers; time pressure moderate; narrative focus on discovery (father's letter, Walter Smith meeting)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interior temple corridors; stone architecture; trap rooms; ritual chambers; elevator sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy difficulty escalates; mini-bosses appear; Ryu gains power-ups (special weapons); time limits per level increase; pressure builds toward revelation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Inner temple sanctum; Masked Devil sprite (Ken possessed); crystal orb control mechanism visible; dark mystical palette
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Masked Devil fights Ryu using ninja techniques; Ken under puppet control indicated by possession visual; player must recognize father despite transformation; emotional climax; control mechanism destruction triggers liberation

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Act 6 - Final Battle with Jaquio and Jashin
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temple collapse animation; fiery projectiles from Jaquio; demon resurrection via lunar eclipse visual; Jashin demon sprite (largest boss); destruction sequences
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Jaquio highly aggressive; uses fire attacks and environmental hazards; Jashin demonic form requires extended combat; Ken sacrifices himself (visual death scene); temple destruction forces escape sequence

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Sunrise palette; bright sky; temple ruins receding; Irene and Ryu together; kiss finale; sun rising over ocean
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Irene betrays CIA orders (reflects player choice to ally with her); affection shown; peaceful resolution tone; 'darkness is finally over' dialogue confirms world state shift to safety

world_state_to_visual.world_state: High world danger (bosses alive, statues separated)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Red/orange tones in boss areas; enemy health meters visible; projectile trails; enemy spawn rates high
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies relentless; boss attacks increase frequency; Ryu must chain combos; minimal rest periods; powerup scarcity emphasizes scarcity

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Low world danger (enemies cleared, powerups active)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Calmer color palette; empty corridors; special weapon effects (fire wheel glow, star projectiles, windmill spin); invincibility aura
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ryu moves faster with special weapons; attack combos uninterrupted; enemy respawn delays; player offensive advantage; momentum builds

environmental_storytelling.example: Opening reveals Ryu finding father Ken's letter; character relationships established through dialogue; Jaquio's world domination plan explained via exposition cutscenes

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss identity reveals through visual design
environmental_storytelling.example: Masked Devil sprite is Ken but visually corrupted/changed, signaling possession without explicit text; Jaquio's true form appears only in final chamber, suggesting hidden threat; Bloody Malth's appearance links to father Ken's duel backstory

Ascending from jungle exterior through increasingly ornate temple chambers mirrors Ryu's journey from ignorance to understanding; inner sanctum design reflects mystical power concentration; crystal orb placement in ceremonial chamber emphasizes control mechanism

Falling platforms increase in later acts showing world destabilization; temple collapse sequence during final battle visualizes stakes escalation; lunar eclipse triggers demon awakening, connecting celestial phenomena to narrative climax

Special weapons (Fire Wheel, Throwing Stars, Windmill Star) appear in acts matching escalating difficulty; invincible fire wheel pickup signals final boss preparation; higher placement in Japanese version reflects intentional pacing design

environmental_storytelling.technique: NPC appearance and removal as world-state change
environmental_storytelling.example: Walter Smith's death signals stakes escalation; Irene Lew's character arc (kidnapper→ally→romantic interest) shown through visual interactions; Foster's satellite communication emphasizes external political threat

Title changed to 'Shadow Warriors' in PAL regions due to ninja controversy; Japanese acts named (Destiny, Escape, Pursuit, Trap, Life-or-death Struggle, Conquering the Devil) removed in international versions, but thematic intent preserved through level design; father's name Ken→Joe creates identity shift in localization

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Single katana attacks; wall-climb ability (cling to wall mechanic at RAM 0x0084); standard jump physics; walking/running speed at RAM 0x00AC-0x00AD; first sword attack always ineffective (RAM 0x0092: $00 until sword use, then $10)

Secondary weapon projectiles deployed (Throwing Stars, Fire Wheel, Windmill Star) selected via RAM 0x00C9; attack animation changes; movement speed may increase with special abilities (Jump and Slash); invincible fire wheel provides temporary damage immunity with visual aura
Knockback animation; invulnerability flash (RAM 0x0095 runs 60-0 frames); health meter depletes (RAM 0x0065); if HP reaches 0, life lost; brief protection window allows escape repositioning

Sprite animation triggers; frames until action increments (RAM 0x0408-0x040F); horizontal movement velocity set (RAM 0x0448-0x0457, 0x0450-0x0457); facing direction determined (RAM 0x0440-0x0447: left/right); projectile throw or charging behavior initiated

Y-position adjusts to follow Ryu's Y (memory 0x8A) — if bird Y > Ryu Y, bird moves up (-1); if < Ryu Y, bird moves down (+1); X-position and speed adjust to follow Ryu's X (similar convergence logic); predictable tracking curve enables player manipulation (per TASVideos bird trajectory prediction script)

Boss-specific HP tracked separately (RAM 0x0490-0x0497 for active boss); health meter displayed (RAM 0x0066); attack patterns cycle based on frames until action; projectile or melee attacks launch at intervals; defeat triggers explosion animation (RAM 0x04B8-0x04BA) and act completion

Fights with father's ninja techniques (implies advanced combat behavior); visual corruption/darkened sprite indicates possession; eyes show puppet-like control (per narrative dialogue); destroying the control crystal orb (placed in chamber) breaks hold and returns Ken to normal state

Throws fiery projectiles as attack signature; moves unpredictably in chamber; dialogue taunts Ryu; upon defeat, triggers lunar eclipse event that resurrects Jashin; throws Ken through trapdoor earlier, showing territorial aggression

Largest sprite form; intense attack patterns; longest boss health meter; requires sustained player offense; defeat ends game's primary threat; visual destruction sequence signals world-state resolution

Initially kidnaps Ryu (betrayal signal); later aids Ryu in temple (alliance signal); final state shows romantic attraction (kiss scene); rejects CIA order to kill Ryu (defection confirmed); sunrise scene shows alignment with Ryu's faction, not Jaquio's or Foster's

notes: Ninja Gaiden NES uses cinematic cutscenes as primary narrative delivery, with environmental and sprite-based visual cues to communicate danger, faction allegiance, and world-state shifts. Danger is primarily signaled through enemy spawning, boss health meters, and time pressure (level timer). Safety is indicated by level completion transitions and power-up acquisition. The game employs regional localization (title change to 'Shadow Warriors' in PAL, character name changes, act title removal) that reflects geopolitical context of the late 1980s ninja-related sensitivities in UK/EU. Behavioral tells for entities are tightly coupled to RAM state — enemy position, velocity, and action timing all derive from specific memory addresses, making the game's state communication highly data-driven. The possessed father (Masked Devil) serves as emotional/narrative danger signal distinct from mechanical threats. The lunar eclipse acts as a world-state trigger event, transforming defeat into victory condition through demon resurrection mechanic.
